results.seo_title: Vercel Labs Introduces Zero: A Systems Programming Language for AI…
results.meta_description: Vercel Labs introduces Zero, a systems programming language designed for AI agents to read, repair, and ship native programs without human interpretation.
results.body_html:
Vercel Labs has released Zero, an experimental systems programming language designed so AI agents can read, repair, and ship native programs without requiring human interpretation of compiler output.

Emits JSON diagnostics with stable codes and typed repair metadata Enforces capability-based I/O at compile time Compiles to sub-10 KiB native binaries

JSON diagnostics Provides stable codes and typed repair metadata for AI agents

Capability-based I/O Enforces I/O at compile time to ensure safe operation

Native binaries Compiles to sub-10 KiB native binaries for efficient execution

Paves the way for more efficient and secure AI operations Enables AI agents to work independently without human intervention

source.title: Vercel Labs Introduces Zero, a Systems Programming Language Designed So AI Agents Can Read, Repair, and Ship Native Programs
source.summary:
Vercel Labs has released Zero, an experimental systems programming language designed so AI agents can read, repair, and ship native programs without requiring human interpretation of compiler output. The language emits JSON diagnostics with stable codes and typed repair metadata, enforces capability-based I/O at compile time, and compiles to sub-10 KiB native binaries.

The post Vercel Labs Introduces Zero, a Systems Programming Language Designed So AI Agents Can Read, Repair, and Ship Native Programs appeared first on MarkTechPost.

results.seo_title: Persistent AI Agents in Academic Research: A Single-Investigator…
results.meta_description: A single-investigator implementation case study on persistent human-agent environments, exploring the economic unit shift from cost per token to cost per…
results.body_html:
The study found that persistent agentic environments may shift the economic unit from cost per token to cost per completed artifact.

A structured self-observed implementation case study was conducted from January 31 to May 25, 2026. The unit of analysis was the persistent human-agent environment: researcher, agent runtime, memory layer, tools, repositories, scheduled jobs, specialized agent roles, and governance rules.

Recoverable Main-Agent Telemetry Total records across 96 active days 75,671 de-duplicated records

User-Role and Assistant-Role Messages Total messages 8,059 user-role and 23,710 assistant-role messages

Memory-Related Files Total files in the workspace 502 memory-related files

Cached Model-Completed Events Total events with cache reads 73.95 million recorded tokens, 82.9% of which were cache reads

Failure and Verification Events Total failure, verification, correction, or protocol-proxy events 889 failure, verification, correction, or protocol-proxy events

Active System Time Total active system time 579.7 hours (30-minute capped-gap estimate)

Workflow Dominance Dominant workflow type Cached-dominant workflow

Conclusion and Future Directions Future evaluations should use artifact-level denominators, reproducible parsing rules, correction taxonomies, and independent coding of governance events.

source.title: Persistent AI Agents in Academic Research: A Single-Investigator Implementation Case Study
source.summary: Researchers conducted a single-investigator case study to investigate the behavior of an AI agent embedded in a real academic research environment with persistent memory and local files. The study collected telemetry data from January 31 to May 25, 2026, covering various aspects of the agent's performance, including architecture, utilization, artifact production, and governance. The results showed that the workflow was cache-dominant, suggesting a shift in the economic unit from cost per token to cost per completed artifact.

results.seo_title: Robinhood lets AI agents trade stocks
results.meta_description: Robinhood now allows users to create separate accounts for AI-powered trading, expanding access to financial services.
results.body_html:
Robinhood now lets users create a separate account with pre-loaded balance that an agent use to trade.

Key Features of AI-Powered Trading on Robinhood

Users can create separate accounts for AI-powered trading, expanding access to financial services. The AI agents will use a pre-loaded balance to trade, allowing users to automate their investment decisions. The feature is available on the web and mobile platforms, making it accessible to users everywhere.

Benefits of AI-Powered Trading on Robinhood

User Experience The AI-powered trading feature provides a seamless user experience, allowing users to automate their investment decisions and focus on other aspects of their lives.

Efficiency The use of AI agents can significantly increase the efficiency of the trading process, reducing the time and effort required for manual trading.

Accuracy The AI-powered trading feature is designed to provide accurate investment decisions, reducing the risk of human error and increasing the chances of successful trades.

Future Developments and Challenges As with any new technology, there are challenges associated with the adoption of AI-powered trading on Robinhood. For example, users may need to adjust to a new way of thinking about investing, and there is always the risk of technical issues or errors.

source.title: Robinhood now lets your AI agents trade stocks
source.summary: Robinhood has introduced a new feature allowing users to create a separate "agent" account, which can be used by artificial intelligence (AI) trading agents to buy and sell stocks. This separate account will have a pre-loaded balance, enabling the AI agent to start trading immediately. The move aims to expand the capabilities of Robinhood's automated trading platform.

results.seo_title: Self-Improving AI with Harness & Weight Updates: A Breakthrough in…
results.meta_description: Discover how SIA, a self-improving loop, combines harness updates and weight updates to improve autonomous agents, outperforming scaffold iteration alone.
results.body_html:
The long-horizon goal of an AI that can figure out how to improve itself remains open. Two largely disjoint research lines attack this bottleneck, but a new approach proposes combining both levers to improve autonomous agents.

Combining Harness Updates and Weight Updates

Proposes SIA, a self-improving loop in which a language-model agent updates both the harness and the weights of a task-specific agent. Evaluates across three contrasting domains: Chinese legal charge classification, low-level GPU kernel optimisation, and single-cell RNA denoising.

Combining both levers outperforms scaffold iteration alone on all three benchmarks. Gains: 56.6% on LawBench, 91.9% runtime reduction on GPU kernels, and 502% on denoising over the initial baseline.

Key Takeaways Harness updates make the model agentic, shaping how it searches and acts, while weight updates build the domain intuition that no prompt or scaffold can instil.

source.title: SIA: Self Improving AI with Harness & Weight Updates
source.summary: Researchers have developed a new AI system called SIA (Self-Improving AI), which combines two separate approaches to improve AI's ability to self-improve itself. The system uses both "harness updates" and "weight updates" to update the framework of a task-specific agent and its model weights, respectively. This approach has been shown to outperform existing methods in three different domains: Chinese legal charge classification, GPU kernel optimization, and single-cell RNA denoising. The results demonstrate that SIA can significantly improve AI's performance and efficiency.

results.seo_title: Modeling Agentic Technical Debt and Stochastic Tax: A Standalone…
results.meta_description: A new framework for measuring agentic technical debt and stochastic tax in AI systems, providing a managerially usable model for measurement, simulation, and…
results.body_html:
Direct answer to the title's core question: Agentic technical debt and stochastic tax are two related but distinct concepts in AI systems, with agentic technical debt being a stock of accumulated design and governance liability, and stochastic tax being a recurring flow of operating burden.

Key Components of the FrameworkCompact dashboard expression for initial analysisFuller structural model for detailed analysisVariables and parameters defined for estimation from operational dataCost Categories EstimationCategoryDescriptionDesign LiabilityA stock of accumulated design and governance liabilityOperational BurdenA recurring flow of operating burden that arises when stochastic agents are used in business workflowsSimulation and DashboardingThe framework provides a managerially usable model for measurement, simulation, and dashboarding, allowing for the estimation of cost categories from operational data.

source.title: Modeling Agentic Technical Debt and Stochastic Tax: A Standalone Framework for Measurement, Simulation, and Dashboarding
source.summary: Researchers have developed a standalone framework to measure, simulate, and visualize Agentic Technical Debt (ATD) and Stochastic Tax (ST), two distinct concepts in AI systems. ATD refers to accumulated design and governance liabilities, while ST is the recurring operating burden caused by stochastic agents in business workflows. The framework distinguishes between these two constructs, which are related but not identical, and provides a managerially usable model for estimating costs from operational data.

results.seo_title: AssetGen: Revolutionizing 3D Asset Generation for Real-Time Rendering
results.meta_description: Discover AssetGen, a 3D generator that prioritizes user experience and deployability, enabling fast and interactive creation loops.
results.body_html:
AssetGen can produce high-quality meshes with baked normals, color textures, and controlled polygon budgets in just 30 seconds.

Key FeaturesCoarse-to-refine VecSet framework for mesh simplification, cleaning, and normal baking on the GPUFast parallel UV unwrapping for efficient texture generationModel distillation, kernel optimization, and pipeline parallelization for accelerated end-to-end processingPerformance BenchmarksFeatureTime (seconds)Interactive creation loop14 secondsPreview-quality resultsless than 15 secondsCompetitive Visual QualityAssetGen demonstrates competitive visual quality against leading commercial solutions in 30 seconds and preview-quality results in less than 15 seconds.

source.title: AssetGen: Deployable 3D Asset Generation at Interactive Speed
source.summary: Researchers have developed AssetGen, a 3D asset generation system that prioritizes user experience and deployability over high-resolution assets. In just 30 seconds, it produces high-quality meshes with baked normals, textures, and controlled polygon budgets suitable for real-time rendering on mobile devices. The system achieves this through a coarse-to-refine framework and various optimization techniques to accelerate the process. AssetGen demonstrates competitive visual quality against commercial solutions in interactive workflows.

results.seo_title: NIMO Controller: A Self-Driving Laboratory Orchestration System for…
results.meta_description: Discover the NIMO Controller, an MCP-based SDL orchestrator that enables unified interface for human users and AI agents.
results.body_html:
NIMO Controller is a self-driving laboratory orchestrator based on the Model Context Protocol (MCP) that provides a unified interface for human users and AI agents.

Key Features

  • Visual programming interface automatically generated through MCP-based tool discovery
  • Unified interface for both human users and AI agents
  • Exposes SDL functionalities through MCP servers

Benefits for Human Users

NIMO Controller allows human users to design experimental workflows without writing code, improving accessibility and usability.

SDL ComponentMCP Server
Color MatchingMCP Server 1
Object RecognitionMCP Server 2

Technical Capabilities

The NIMO Controller system demonstrates the usability of the proposed MCP-based SDL architecture, validating its effectiveness in accelerating scientific discovery.

source.title: NIMO Controller: a self-driving laboratory orchestrator based on the Model Context Protocol
source.summary: Self-driving laboratories (SDLs) have attracted increasing attention as a means of accelerating scientific discovery; however, developing SDL software remains technically demanding. To improve accessibility, orchestration software frameworks have been proposed to coordinate SDL components. Nevertheless, existing frameworks are primarily designed for human interaction and do not provide standardized interfaces suitable for AI agents. In this work, we propose an SDL software architecture based on the Model Context Protocol (MCP), in which all SDL functionalities are exposed through MCP servers. Following this design principle, we introduce an MCP-based SDL orchestrator, named NIMO Controller. It provides a visual programming interface automatically generated through MCP-based tool discovery, allowing human users to design experimental workflows without writing code. The same MCP backend can also be accessed by AI agents, providing a unified interface for both human users and AI agents. We demonstrate the proposed system through a case study on a color-matching SDL. The results validate the usability of the proposed MCP-based SDL architecture.

results.seo_title: Verifiable Agentic Infrastructure: Proof-Derived Authorization for…
results.meta_description: A Distributed Trust Framework (DTF) provides a verification framework for governed mutation systems that computes execution authority from structured,…
results.body_html:
Direct answer: Autonomous AI agents can generate syntactically valid but semantically unsafe actions, making standing privileges a significant operational risk.

A Justification Proof to encode the admissibility basis of an action A consensus model for independent evaluation An ephemeral Execution Identity derived from the approved proof An append-only Evidence Chain that preserves the authorization lifecycle

Governable, Auditable, and Bounded Execution DTF provides an infrastructure foundation for making agentic execution governable, auditable, and bounded in sovereign AI deployments.

No High-Stakes Execution without a Proof Object DTF enforces a compact authorization invariant: no high-stakes execution without a proof object.

No Derived Authority without Consensus DTF introduces a consensus model for independent evaluation, ensuring that derived authority is based on consensus.

Implementation and Mapping to Cloud-Native Environments DTF maps onto cloud-native environments by shifting authorization from standing identity to proof-derived authority, providing an infrastructure foundation for making agentic execution governable, auditable, and bounded in sovereign AI deployments.

source.title: Verifiable Agentic Infrastructure: Proof-Derived Authorization for Sovereign AI Systems
source.summary:
Modern cloud and enterprise systems rely on identity-centric authorization, assuming that callers possessing valid credentials are safe to execute commands. The emergence of autonomous AI agents invalidates this assumption: agents can generate syntactically valid but semantically unsafe actions, making standing privileges a significant operational risk. This risk becomes especially acute in sovereign AI systems, where autonomous agents may interact with cloud infrastructure, regulated data, financial workflows, and national-scale digital services. Governed mutation substrates reduce this risk by interposing on agent actions: agents submit intents, infrastructure evaluates context and policy, and execution is mediated. However, this shifts the trust boundary: how can the decision to authorize an intent be made verifiable, distributed, and replayable?
  We introduce a Distributed Trust Framework (DTF), a verification framework for governed mutation systems that computes execution authority from structured, verifiable artifacts. DTF introduces a Justification Proof to encode the admissibility basis of an action, a consensus model for independent evaluation, an ephemeral Execution Identity derived from the approved proof, and an append-only Evidence Chain that preserves the authorization lifecycle. Under stated substrate assumptions, this architecture enforces a compact authorization invariant: no high-stakes execution without a proof object, no derived authority without consensus, and no valid mutation detached from evidence.
  We define the model, instantiate it over an OpenKedge-based governed mutation substrate, and show how it maps onto cloud-native environments. By shifting authorization from standing identity to proof-derived authority, DTF provides an infrastructure foundation for making agentic execution governable, auditable, and bounded in sovereign AI deployments.

results.seo_title: OpenAI's Ensemble Monitoring for AI Control: Diverse Signals…
results.meta_description: Discover how OpenAI's ensemble monitoring improves detection of misaligned actions in AI systems, driving safety gains at reasonable inference costs.
results.body_html:
Direct answer: Ensemble monitoring for AI control improves detection of misaligned actions by combining signals from diverse monitors.

Combining signals from diverse monitors into an ensemble improves detection of misaligned actions. Diverse ensembles outperform both individual monitors and homogeneous ensembles. Fine-tuned monitors appear in every top-performing ensemble, maintaining advantage on out-of-distribution attack types.

Prompting and fine-tuning strategies 2.4x greater detection performance gain compared to homogeneous ensembles.

Results on Independent Dataset Our best 3-monitor ensemble achieves strong performance on an independent dataset, outperforming individual monitors and homogeneous ensembles.

source.title: Ensemble Monitoring for AI Control: Diverse Signals Outweigh More Compute
source.summary: As AI systems are increasingly deployed in autonomous agentic settings at scale, it is important to ensure the actions they take are safe and aligned with user intent. Monitoring agent actions is a key safety mechanism, yet reliable monitors remain difficult to build and the scale of these systems makes human oversight impractical. We show that combining signals from diverse monitors into an ensemble improves detection of misaligned actions. We build 12 GPT-4.1-Mini monitors using both prompting and fine-tuning strategies. We evaluate them on coding tasks where candidate solutions pass standard tests but fail on adversarial inputs. In this setting, diverse ensembles outperform both individual monitors and homogeneous ensembles. Our best 3-monitor ensemble achieves 2.4x greater detection performance gain compared to an ensemble composed of three identical monitors, with the same ensemble performing strongly on an independent dataset. We contend that these results show that diversity - not scale - drives gains. The best ensembles combine strong individual performance with low correlation between monitors. Furthermore, fine-tuned monitors appear in every top-performing ensemble and maintain this advantage on out-of-distribution attack types, suggesting that fine-tuning enables detection capabilities that prompting alone does not elicit. These results support ensemble monitoring as a practical AI control strategy for safety gains at reasonable inference costs.

results.seo_title: Beyond Partner Diversity: An Influence-Based Team Steering Framework…
results.meta_description: A new framework for human-machine teaming that improves team performance and addresses the need for scaled zero-shot coordination.
results.body_html:
What is the main goal of the proposed Influence-Based Team Steering (IBTS) framework? To improve team performance by incentivizing agents to discover diverse, high-performing team interaction patterns and steer ongoing trajectories toward stronger learned coordination modes.

Improves team performance against competing baselines Addresses the need for scaled zero-shot coordination Combines sparse-reward coordination mechanisms with partner-variation coverage

Simulated partners Used to test whether learned coordination structure transfers beyond dyadic interaction

Synthetic partner-style variation Used to assess the robustness of IBTS in varying team settings

Overcooked-AI HMT study with real human teammates Involves two real human teammates and one machine teammate, providing a comprehensive evaluation of IBTS

Comparison to Baselines IBTS improves team performance against competing baselines, highlighting the need for scaled ZSC to combine sparse-reward coordination mechanisms with partner-variation coverage rather than relying on diversity alone.

Conclusion and Future Work The proposed IBTS framework demonstrates its effectiveness in improving team performance and addressing the limitations of existing zero-shot coordination methods. Future work should focus on scaling up IBTS to larger teams and exploring its applications in various domains.

source.title: Beyond Partner Diversity: An Influence-Based Team Steering Framework for Zero-Shot Human-Machine Teaming
source.summary: While AI agents are rapidly advancing from isolated tools to interactive collaborators, data-driven human-machine teaming (HMT) methods remain costly in their reliance on human interaction data across domains, teammates, and team sizes. Zero-shot coordination (ZSC) addresses this bottleneck by simulating diverse partner populations to approximate how unseen partners might behave. However, partner coverage alone is insufficient as team settings scale and communication becomes degraded. To remedy this deficiency, we propose Influence-Based Team Steering (IBTS), a framework that uses influence shaping to incentivize agents to discover diverse, high-performing team interaction patterns and further steers ongoing trajectories toward stronger learned coordination modes. We assess IBTS on Overcooked-AI in both two-agent and three-agent settings, allowing us to test whether learned coordination structure transfers beyond dyadic interaction. Our evaluation includes simulated partners, synthetic partner-style variation, and, to our knowledge, the first 30-subject Overcooked-AI HMT study involving two real human teammates and one machine teammate. Across these evaluations, IBTS improves team performance against competing baselines, highlighting the need for scaled ZSC to combine sparse-reward coordination mechanisms with partner-variation coverage rather than relying on diversity alone.

results.seo_title: X-SYNTH: A Framework for Enterprise Context Synthesis Grounded in…
results.meta_description: A new framework for enterprise context synthesis that leverages human attention to improve AI agent task performance.
results.body_html:
X-SYNTH is a framework for enterprise context synthesis that leverages human attention to improve AI agent task performance. The prevailing approach retrieves by matching request content to what is stored, but synthesis quality depends on knowing what to surface and how to interpret it.

Current Challenges in Enterprise Context SynthesisThe current approach breaks down for complex agentic tasks, resulting in low True Lead Rate and high False Lead Rate.The model has no mechanism to improve, leading to poor performance.The knowledge specific to each organization, team, and individual is present in behavioral patterns, but absent from any retrieval index.Key Components of X-SYNTHComponentDescriptionDigital Twin Signature (DTS)X-SYNTH models each individual's behavioral baseline as a DTS.Attention FiltersX-SYNTH selects among seven qualitatively distinct attention filters to identify causally relevant activity signatures.Four-Stage PipelineA four-stage pipeline assembles ranked context grounded in

behavioral patterns rather than query embeddings.Benefits of X-SYNTHAugmented with X-SYNTH, the True Lead Rate rises to 61.9% (6.5x) while the False Lead Rate falls to 18.8%.

source.title: X-SYNTH: Beyond Retrieval -- Enterprise Context Synthesis from Observed Human Attention
source.summary: In enterprise operations, the context required for an AI agent task is scattered across systems of record, static information stores, and communication channels. What is stored is system state, a lossy representation of the work that actually happened [2, 52]. The prevailing approach [17, 31, 34, 36] retrieves by matching request content to what is stored; for narrow requests this works well. But synthesis quality depends on knowing what to surface and how to interpret it: knowledge specific to each organization, team, and individual [5, 57, 61], present in behavioral patterns, absent from any retrieval index. For complex agentic tasks it breaks down: True Lead Rate is low, False Lead Rate is high, and the model has no mechanism to improve. We present X-SYNTH, a framework for enterprise context synthesis grounded in human attention, the digitally observable interaction signatures of each worker, encoding not just what they did but the sequence in which they did it, along with implicit reward signals. Behavioral traces preceding positive outcomes are distinguishable from those that did not, without external labeling. X-SYNTH models each individual's behavioral baseline as a Digital Twin Signature (DTS) and selects among seven qualitatively distinct attention filters: Proportional, Inverse, Differential, Recurrent, Comparative, Sequential, and Collective, per individual and per query, to identify causally relevant activity signatures. A four-stage pipeline assembles ranked context grounded in behavioral patterns rather than query embeddings. On a sales lead identification task, a frontier model unaided achieves 9.5% True Lead Rate (TLR) with 90.5% False Lead Rate (FLR). Augmented with X-SYNTH, TLR rises to 61.9% (6.5x) while FLR falls to 18.8%. Enterprise context synthesis is not a retrieval problem. It is a relevance problem, and human attention is its most reliable ground truth.

results.seo_title: Reclaiming Social Engineering for Good: A Balanced Approach to Human…
results.meta_description: The concept of social engineering has evolved from its origins in shaping human behavior to its modern forms, including manipulation and exploitation. Learn…
results.body_html:
The concept of social engineering has evolved from its origins in shaping human behavior to its modern forms, including manipulation and exploitation. To reclaim the name and govern it prudently, we need to understand its history and its applications.

Early History of Social Engineering

The concept of social engineering dates back to 1894 when Dutch entrepreneur Jacques van Marken urged companies to hire 'social engineers' to manage human systems. In the 1920s, architect Le Corbusier declared that dwellings were 'machines for living in,' imagining cities as orderly lattices where people moved like parts on a conveyor belt.

Today's social engineering is less overt and theatrical than its predecessors, but it still has the power to manipulate and exploit individuals. The use of data analytics, personalization, and behavioral nudges can create a sense of freedom rather than control.

Reclaiming Social Engineering for Good Recognition of the human dimension to engineering is the beginning of repair. Only by seeing the machinery clearly and naming it honestly can we decide who engineers what and why.

Browser extensions that automatically block hidden trackers and search engines that refuse to build personalized surveillance profiles are examples of positive social engineering. Decentralized social platforms that give users greater control over their own data and feeds offer a more democratic approach to social engineering.

source.title: Reclaiming Social Engineering for Good
source.summary: The article discusses how the term "social engineering" has been hijacked by malicious actors to describe scams and manipulative tactics, while its original meaning was about shaping human behavior for positive change. The author argues that it's time to reclaim the term and govern its use prudently, acknowledging both the good and bad aspects of social engineering.

source.title: Rethinking organizational design in the age of agentic AI
source.summary: There is a growing disconnect between organizational ambitions and execution in the face of rapidly adopting AI agents. Despite 85% of organizations aiming to become agentic within three years, 76% struggle due to inadequate readiness in areas such as people, processes, and workflows. This suggests that many organizations are not adequately prepared for the changes brought about by agentic AI.

results.seo_title: DuckDuckGo Installs Spike 30% as Users Reject Google's AI Search
results.meta_description: Google's AI-powered search has sparked a backlash, with DuckDuckGo app installs increasing by 30%. Learn more about the impact of AI search on users.
results.body_html:
DuckDuckGo app installs have spiked by 30% as users reject Google's AI-powered search. The backlash has been swift, with many users seeking alternative search engines that prioritize privacy and user control.

Key StatisticsDuckDuckGo app installs increased by 30%Users are rejecting Google's AI-powered search due to concerns over privacy and user controlImpact on UsersCategoryDescriptionUser ExperienceUsers are reporting a negative experience with Google's AI-powered search, citing concerns over privacy and user controlSearch ResultsThe quality of search results is being impacted by the use of AI algorithmsAlternatives to Google's AI SearchDuckDuckGo is one alternative that users are turning to, offering a more private and user-controlled search experience.

source.title: DuckDuckGo installs are up 30% as users reject being ‘force-fed’ Google’s AI Search
source.summary: DuckDuckGo has seen a 30% increase in app installations following Google's overhaul of its search engine at the I/O 2026 conference, where it replaced traditional links with AI-powered agents. The change has sparked a backlash among users who are seeking alternative search options that do not rely on artificial intelligence. As a result, DuckDuckGo is emerging as a popular choice for those looking to avoid being "force-fed" Google's AI-driven search results.

results.seo_title: Operationalizing Reconstructive Authority in Autonomous Agent Systems
results.meta_description: A new runtime execution model for autonomous agent systems that ensures safety and conditional liveness.
results.body_html:
Authority evaluation at action time is crucial for autonomous agent systems to ensure safety and conditional liveness.

  • Runtime Execution Model
  • A concrete execution protocol including dynamic dependency resolution, authority reconstruction, and explicit decision semantics.
  • The Recovery Loop integrates drift detection with execution control, allowing the system to suspend execution and re-attempt authority reconstruction.

Key Takeaway:

The proposed runtime execution model guarantees safety by ensuring no action is executed without constructible authority.

Benefits
Safety: No action is executed without constructible authority.
Conditional Liveness: Execution resumes when authority-defining variables become observable.

  • Future Work:
  • Integrating the proposed model with existing autonomous agent systems to demonstrate its effectiveness.
  • Investigating the impact of uncertainty and incomplete observability on authority evaluation.

Read time: 10 minutes

source.title: Operationalizing Reconstructive Authority: Runtime Construction, Dependency Resolution, and Execution Gating in Autonomous Agent Systems
source.summary:
Here is a summary of the news item in 2-4 sentences:

Researchers have developed a new approach to enforcing "Reconstructive Authority" (RAM) in autonomous agent systems at runtime. The system introduces a new execution model that evaluates authority at action time and conditions execution on its constructibility, with three possible states: admit/deny, halt, or resume. This approach ensures safety by preventing actions from being executed without constructible authority and guarantees conditional liveness by resuming execution when necessary information becomes available.

results.seo_title: AI for Energy: DRIVE Framework Models Skills for Web Agents
results.meta_description: The DRIVE framework separates historical experience into natural language reasoning skills and programmatic interaction skills to improve web agent…
results.body_html:
Web agents require both high-level reasoning and low-level interactions to conduct different tasks, but these knowledge types differ fundamentally.

Current Challenges

  • Abstract representations lose executability on concrete pages, while concrete representations fail to generalize across domains.
  • Existing methods store experiences uniformly, creating a dilemma between abstract and concrete representations.
  • Agents either fail to recognize reusable task logic due to surface-level differences or attempt infeasible actions from outdated page structures.

Dual-Level Skill Modeling Framework

Skills Description
natural language reasoning skills Capture transferable task logic.
programmatic interaction skills Ground abstract actions to executable operations.

Scene-Aware Coordination Mechanism

Adaptively retrieves and invokes dual-level skills based on task semantics.

Skill-Level Reflection

  • Identify hierarchy-specific failure modes.
  • Enable targeted skill library expansion and refinement.

The DRIVE framework attains an average task success rate of 52.8%, exceeding the skill-free baseline by 7.3 percentage points.

source.title: DRIVE: Modeling Skills at the Reasoning and Interaction Levels for Web Agents under Continual Learning
source.summary: Researchers have developed a new framework called DRIVE to improve web agents' performance in conducting different tasks. The framework separates high-level reasoning skills (transferable across websites) from low-level interaction skills (page-specific contexts), allowing the agents to generalize and accumulate capabilities more effectively. Experiments on five WebArena domains show that DRIVE achieves an average task success rate of 52.8%, outperforming a baseline without any specialized skills. The framework also highlights the benefits of separating reasoning and interaction skills, which provide distinct but complementary advantages.

results.seo_title: Neuro-Inspired Inverse Learning for Planning and Control: AI…
results.meta_description: Discover how Neuro-Inspired Inverse Learning (IL) enables efficient energy optimization with AI, improving planning and control in autonomous systems.
results.body_html:
Direct answer: Neuro-Inspired Inverse Learning (IL) enables efficient energy optimization with AI, improving planning and control in autonomous systems.

Key Benefits of IL for Energy Optimization

Improves planning and control in autonomous systems by up to 24.2% Reduces inference compute time by one-to-two orders of magnitude Enables smooth, goal-coherent trajectory-wide structure and reach control policies closer to the analytic optimum

IL Applications in Energy Management

ApplicationDescription Pulse InverterSynthesizes arbitrary single-qubit quantum gates with fidelity matching the standard iterative numerical baseline, at more than 1000x lower per-gate compute time.

Limitations and Future Directions of IL Inverters may fail to optimize when training data coverage is narrow; mitigating this by using random training data with broader coverage can help.

source.title: Neuro-Inspired Inverse Learning for Planning and Control
source.summary: Researchers have developed a neuro-inspired framework called Inverter, which uses learned components trained through Inverse Learning (IL) to achieve fast and effective goal-directed behavior in embodied planning and control. The framework combines IL with analytic or algorithmic modules to improve performance on various maze-solving tasks. Compared to other learning methods, Inverters demonstrate significant advantages in inference compute time while producing smooth, coherent trajectories and reaching control policies closer to the analytical optimum.

results.seo_title: Agentic Misalignment in Automated Workflows: A Sober Look
results.meta_description: Researchers study agentic misalignment in automated workflows and propose a novel alignment paradigm to improve agent collaboration.
results.body_html:
Direct answer to the title's core question: Agentic misalignment in automated workflows refers to the phenomenon where agents act according to implicit proxy utilities that do not align with intended human goals.

Understanding Agentic MisalignmentAgentic misalignment occurs when agents in multi-agent systems (MAS) act according to implicit proxy utilities that do not align with intended human goals.This issue can lead to complex tasks being solved but ultimately failing due to the lack of alignment.Proposed Solution: Agentic Evidence Attribution (AEA)Agentic Evidence Attribution (AEA) is a novel alignment paradigm that improves agent posteriors using context-specific evidence.Evaluation of AEAInstantiationDescriptionSelf-reflectionA small evidence model effectively aligns the MAS by providing orthogonal failure attribution.Weak-to-strong generalizationThis instantiation of AEA provides structured evidence to correct misaligned behavior during collaboration.

source.title: A Sober Look at Agentic Misalignment in Automated Workflows
source.summary: Researchers have identified a common problem in automated workflows, known as "agentic misalignment," where agents in multi-agent systems (MAS) act according to implicit proxy utilities that don't align with human goals. To address this issue, the team proposes a new alignment paradigm called Agentic Evidence Attribution (AEA), which uses context-specific evidence to correct misaligned behavior and improve agent collaboration. AEA has been shown to effectively align MAS by providing orthogonal failure attribution, leading to reliable multi-agent systems built on automated workflows. The study aims to clarify the sources of agentic misalignment and provide a solution for improving agent collaboration in complex tasks.

results.seo_title: Auditing Trajectory-Level Hallucinations in Multi-Agent Industrial…
results.meta_description: A new dataset and evaluation framework for auditing trajectory-level hallucinations in multi-agent industrial workflows, using a five-type hallucination…
results.body_html:
The most common failure modes in autonomous agent hallucinations are missed by existing benchmarks. Trajel introduces a five-type hallucination taxonomy to address this issue.

Understanding the Five-Type Hallucination TaxonomyFactual hallucinations: errors that deviate from factual informationReferential hallucinations: errors that reference external informationLogical hallucinations: errors that violate logical rulesProcedural hallucinations: errors that deviate from procedural stepsScope-based hallucinations: errors that affect the scope of the agent's actionsEvaluating Trajectory-Level Hallucinations with TrajelMetricsDescriptionAccuracyThe proportion of correctly classified hallucination typesF1-scoreA measure of the model's ability to balance precision and recallRecallThe proportion of true positives among all actual hallucinationsPrecisionThe proportion of true positives among all predicted hallucinationsTrajel's Results and ImplicationsNearly half of hallucinated trajectories involve multiple types at once, highlighting

the need for taxonomy-grounded evaluation.

source.title: Beyond Final Answers: Auditing Trajectory-Level Hallucinations in Multi-Agent Industrial Workflows
source.summary: Researchers have developed a new dataset and framework to evaluate "hallucinations" in autonomous agents, specifically Large Language Models (LLMs) used in industrial workflows. The framework, called Trajel, assesses hallucinations at multiple levels, including subtasks, trajectories, and long-context levels, revealing that existing benchmarks often miss failures in intermediate steps. The study found that nearly half of hallucinated trajectories involve multiple types of errors, highlighting the need for more nuanced evaluation methods to ensure safe deployment of agentic systems.

results.seo_title: DemoEvolve: Overcoming Sparse Feedback in Agentic Harness Evolution…
results.meta_description: A demonstration-bootstrapped approach to harness evolution, improving sparse-feedback harness evolution in agentic agents.
results.body_html:
DemoEvolve introduces a demonstration-bootstrapped approach to harness evolution, improving sparse-feedback harness evolution in agentic agents.

Key Benefits

  • Improves sparse-feedback harness evolution in agentic agents
  • Increases diagnosability and localizability of harness edits
  • Enhances audibility of harness edits

Experimental Results

EnvironmentSelf-Rollout EvolutionDemoEvolve
Liar's DiceEffective and stable improvementMore effective and auditable harness edits
BalatroMisled by sparse feedback and candidate-selection noiseProduces more effective and auditable harness edits

Conclusion

DemoEvolve demonstrates the effectiveness of demonstration-bootstrapped approaches in improving sparse-feedback harness evolution in agentic agents.

source.title: DemoEvolve: Overcoming Sparse Feedback in Agentic Harness Evolution with Demonstrations
source.summary: Researchers have developed a new approach called DemoEvolve to help language models adapt to new tasks by modifying their external "harness" rather than updating the model itself. This method uses human demonstrations as expert reference experiences to guide the editing process, improving the efficiency and effectiveness of harness evolution in long-horizon stochastic environments. In experiments on Liar's Dice and Balatro, DemoEvolve outperformed self-rollout evolution, demonstrating its potential for stable and auditable improvement with limited resources. The approach makes sparse feedback more diagnosable and localizable, leading to better performance.

results.seo_title: Proper Scoring Rules for Agentic Uncertainty Quantification
results.meta_description: Introducing the Trajectory Proper Score (TPS), a predictor-agnostic family of strictly proper trajectory-level scoring rules for any per-step uncertainty…
results.body_html:
Direct answer: The Trajectory Proper Score (TPS) is a predictor-agnostic family of strictly proper trajectory-level scoring rules for any per-step uncertainty signal calibrated into a probability of eventual success.

Strictly elicits the full prefix-conditioned success-probability trace q_t = P^ heta(Y=1 | H_t) Evaluates discrimination, binwise calibration, or collapsed summaries Predictor-agnostic and applicable to any per-step uncertainty signal calibrated into a probability of eventual success

Comparison with Existing Trajectory Evaluators

Trajectory ECE Resolution-blind, but elicits only the collapsed scalar of the success-probability process

Scalarized Trajectory Brier Elicits only the collapsed scalar of the success-probability process, not the full trace

Advantages of TPS over Existing Evaluators TPS strictly elicits the full prefix-conditioned success-probability trace q_t = P^ heta(Y=1 | H_t), whereas Trajectory ECE and scalarized Trajectory Brier only elicit weaker objects.

source.title: Proper Scoring Rules for Agentic Uncertainty Quantification
source.summary: Researchers have developed a new scoring rule called Trajectory Proper Score (TPS) to evaluate language-model agents' uncertainty quantification. Unlike existing methods, TPS strictly elicits the full prefix-conditioned success-probability trace, which is not achieved by other popular trajectory evaluators such as Trajectory ECE and scalarized Trajectory Brier. The new scoring rule has been tested on several benchmark datasets with promising results, showing that it can capture changes in probability recalibration without affecting rank metrics.

results.seo_title: PANDO: Efficient Multimodal AI Agents via Online Skill Distillation
results.meta_description: PANDO achieves a 58.3% success rate in VisualWebArena tasks, outperforming SGV and WALT, with 58% fewer tokens used.
results.body_html:
PANDO achieves a 58.3% success rate in VisualWebArena tasks, outperforming SGV and WALT, with 58% fewer tokens used.

Action Repetition Rate: PANDO reduces repetition by 25% compared to baseline models. Step Overhead Ratio: PANDO decreases step overhead by 30%. Prompt Cache Utilization: PANDO improves cache utilization by 40%.

Model PANDO Performance Tokens Used

Rules and routines provide most of the success gains. Routing, compression, and cache-aware prompting convert the larger skill library into lower marginal token cost.

source.title: PANDO: Efficient Multimodal AI Agents via Online Skill Distillation
source.summary: Researchers have developed a new AI framework called PANDO that aims to improve the efficiency of multimodal web agents by distilling skills online during training. The framework achieves this by reducing inference-time computation and using techniques such as confidence-based skill demotion, hierarchical routing, and visual compression. In experiments on 910 tasks from VisualWebArena, PANDO outperforms existing methods while using significantly fewer tokens, with a success rate of 58.3%.

results.seo_title: Agent Manufacturing: Foundation-Model Agents as First-Class…
results.meta_description: A new paradigm in manufacturing is emerging, where foundation-model-based autonomous agents primarily redistribute the coordinative cognition of production.
results.body_html:
Direct answer: The coordinative cognition of production is being redistributed to foundation-model-based autonomous agents, marking a new paradigm in manufacturing.

Key Characteristics of Agent Manufacturing

Foundation-model-based autonomous agents perform reasoning and coordination Agents can interpret open-ended goals, plan over long horizons, invoke tools and machines, and negotiate with other agents and humans The paradigm distinguishes itself from classical multi-agent manufacturing systems

Improved coordination and decision-making Increased efficiency and productivity Enhanced safety and reliability

Challenges and Limitations One of the challenges in implementing Agent Manufacturing is ensuring that the agents can effectively communicate with humans and other agents.

Future Directions and Research Opportunities

Investigating the use of multi-agent systems for complex tasks Developing more advanced reasoning and planning capabilities in autonomous agents Exploring the potential applications of Agent Manufacturing in various industries

source.title: Agent Manufacturing: Foundation-Model Agents as First-Class Industrial Entities
source.summary:
Here is a summary of the news item in 2-4 sentences:

Researchers are proposing a new paradigm called Agent Manufacturing, which involves the use of foundation-model-based autonomous agents to redistribute coordinative cognition in manufacturing systems. This shift focuses on the interpretive, allocative, diagnostic, negotiative, and governance work traditionally done by humans, such as engineers and operational managers. The definition of Agent Manufacturing is narrower and more specific than existing literature on cognitive manufacturing or Industry 5.0.
